page: Microsoft Azure EC64as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor. This CPU features 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz, supported by hyper-threading. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage capabilities. Virtualization is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing and memory capabilities. The server achieves top-tier status in PassMark memory testing, alongside strong performance in multi-core stress-ng, PassMark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory read bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong at 4138.93 GB/sec, though large-block uncached read and write speeds fall into the average category. In database operations and Redis SET operations, the server demonstrates strong throughput. However, large-scale LLM inference is a notable tradeoff, with text generation for a 70B model falling into the weak category at 2.75 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC64as_cc_v5 offers high memory density relative to its vCPU count without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. This makes it a qualitative fit for memory-intensive workloads that utilize network-attached storage. Appropriate workloads include database operations, memory-cached web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, and moderate-scale software compilation.
description: Microsoft Azure EC64as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is configured with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU, but contains no local storage or GPUs. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the server delivers top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core processing capabilities, as evidenced by its top 10% PassMark memory score and top 25% multi-core stress-ng results. Tradeoffs include average uncached large-block memory bandwidth and weak performance in large-model LLM text generation. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy applications that do not require local NVMe drives. It is suited for database operations, Redis caching, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC64as_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized server with a 64-vCPU AMD EPYC processor and 512 GB of RAM. It features top-tier memory performance and is designed for database and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure EC64as_cc_v5 is a 64-vCPU AMD EPYC server with 512 GB RAM, offering top-tier memory performance for database and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and top-tier memory performance for database and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
512.0 GB of RAM providing a memory-optimized 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier PassMark memory performance and strong multi-core processing
No bundled local storage or GPUs, utilizing external Premium Storage
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and multi-threaded workloads
